title: Chapter 206 – Radiant Demon Wing (7)
content:
Xiahou Wei-zhen's eyebrows froze mid-rise, like a bold stroke of dark ink across his brow.

Even after speaking, his mouth hung open, lending him a faintly dazed look.

"I didn't know," he said — plainly, immediately. It sounded almost like an offhand remark.

"Well... I never even considered it," he added.

His deep voice rumbled faintly.

Where once his words had crashed down from above Jeong Yeon-shin's head, now his voice drifted forward, sounding like a murmur pulled from a trance.

It was hardly befitting the man who had stormed the Martial Alliance's pavilions, shattering them to confront family heads.

The extraordinary spirit of a supreme master seemed to dissipate entirely.

Hoo—

A sudden gust surged from the giant clad in black robes. It burst forth in a brief flash, tangled with a wave of energy.

The sunset-bathed ripples on the lake beside them fragmented.

As the water's overlapping waves traced small concentric circles, Xiahou Wei-zhen stamped his foot against the ground, as though anchoring himself.

Boom—

Dust billowed beneath his briefly airborne form.

Whoosh!

He soared with astonishing elasticity. Truly noble clan-like. The texture of energy stretching from the Gushing Spring acupoint in his soles was palpable.

The internal energy current pushing aside the dust was taut yet flexible — elasticity woven through his every motion.

Jeong Yeon-shin's black hair whipped in the breeze beside him.

"He grasped it instantly. Good thing he's perceptive," Jeong Yeon-shin thought.

A single small step had effortlessly lifted that massive frame. Nothing like the domineering fist techniques he had wielded at the Alliance.

No longer the ponderous bearing he once clung to.

Regardless of his bulk, he moved like a noble clansman.

The wide sleeves of his black robes descended then rose lightly, counter to Xiahou Wei-zhen's ascent.

"A skill befitting a three-flowers gathering at crown master. True mastery will require practice," Jeong Yeon-shin mused.

His gaze tracked the arc from apex to landing.

Touching down, Xiahou Wei-zhen's mouth hung slightly open again. His broad brow furrowed deeply.

Shock and bewilderment were plain on his face. His straightforward, honest nature shone through without pretense.

"How could such a secret... You understand the human body better than I do. I never knew the twelve meridians and the full-body muscles held such potential. This... this is worthy of a secret technique. You've taught me something. A profoundly valuable insight, perfectly suited to me."

As the rambling Forest-Treading Leader lifted his foot once more —

"There's no need to jump now..." Jeong Yeon-shin said cautiously.

"Hmm?"

"Didn't you speak of a Leader's decorum at headquarters? You said to mind appearances. Senior Xiahou's conduct lacks a certain refinement — it might look eccentric to passing merchants and officials. You should stop."

"..."

Jeong Yeon-shin took the silence as agreement. While sharing a refined energy pathway, he added practical advice.

This senior seemed to care deeply about black-rank dignity at Desolate Fortress. A favorable impression might serve well.

Xiahou Wei-zhen blinked his large eyes several times. Then he changed the subject.

"Training was a relentless ordeal. My body wasn't built like the clans'. Moving the way our clan moved alone couldn't yield great results, yet abandoning our roots was never an option. I had to think like a sect founder."

A bitter voice filled the air, heavy with years. The decades of hardship required to rise as a Desolate Fortress black-rank seeped through every word.

"I regret the time I spent honing myself without knowing this. Futile. Utterly futile."

"I'm glad to have helped."

Jeong Yeon-shin replied with a straight posture.

He was growing to like Xiahou Wei-zhen quite a bit. Their mutual frankness suited their easygoing natures.

Like the lake smoothed once more into a mirror at their feet — even blunt words would be taken at face value.

And so he continued calmly.

"I understand, since you didn't know. It happens."

His tone was oddly dry.

It sounded, in that moment, thoroughly perfunctory.

In truth, he didn't understand at all.

Like boys his age, he struggled to mask his feelings. Though he believed he hid them well, his manner often stirred irritation instead.

Xiahou Wei-zhen's lips twitched faintly.

He felt a tangle of emotions.

He marveled at a junior so versed in the body that it defied belief. The new Radiant Demon Wing Leader was a veritable master of human anatomy.

The favor filled him with genuine gratitude. And yet, the boy's penchant for righteous proclamations was something else entirely.

That unflinchingly upright demeanor grated on the heart in ways hard to name.

"My fault, perhaps?" Xiahou Wei-zhen thought.

Awareness of his own pettiness turned to shame. He wondered: had he even possessed the right to lecture in the first place? Damn it.

"...Indeed, you've given me great insight. No ordinary revelation. You've opened a new path in movement technique that will affect all my martial arts. If I don't repay this, I'd rightly be called a petty man. I'll yield Kang Chang-moo, the Dawn Tree Sword. A blue-rank from your lineage, I recall."

Desolate Fortress's blue-rank masters already carried a certain renown.

Jeong Yeon-shin had heard of the Dawn Tree Sword.

A swordsman who wielded fierce circular slashes, it was said. And there was a notable tale attached to him.

He had beheaded the Southern Peak Fiend with a blade segmented like dawn shadows.

A covert elimination of an evil faction master who had been intimidating the regional magistrate of Wuchang Prefecture in Huguang Province, extorting luxury and tribute.

"He's now entered the Radiant Sword Style, reaching the eighth level," Jeong Yeon-shin recalled.

He had heard this on the journey from the Alliance to headquarters.

A formidable asset. Though he would not surpass Blue Eyed Demon Sword Cheong Myeong, he could spar and discuss swordsmanship with him meaningfully.

A casual insight had won him a blue-rank master — a feat unlikely through sheer martial strength alone.

Jeong Yeon-shin offered a clasped-fist salute to Xiahou Wei-zhen.

"I'm grateful for your resolve, Senior."

"It's repayment. Repayment."

Xiahou Wei-zhen smiled awkwardly.

At that moment.

Rustle.

Yue Shou-lin, hands clasped behind her back, stepped forward and spun to face them.

Her ebony hair, cascading past her ears, swayed with a lively bounce. Her dark eyes glinted with black radiance.

"One blue-rank won't settle it, will it? Don't you have two white-ranks of Jeong Yeon-shin's lineage under your command?"

"What?"

"The twelve meridians and the full-body minor meridians are wondrous things. A single page mapping those energy paths could spark bloodshed. That's why secret manuals carry such weight — they aren't passed down as faction secrets for nothing."

"..."

"Had our Jeong Yeon-shin not told you, could a dullard like you have grasped such movement on your own? Repay him properly. You should spare no generosity. Unless you'd prefer embarrassment at my hand."

Yue Shou-lin spoke with a smile.

A tale of gifting three swords to the new Radiant Demon Wing Leader. One blue-rank and two white-ranks.

Enough force to dominate a regional martial world without a major faction's backing. A significant bolstering of the Radiant Demon Wing.

"...Your words hold no fault."

Xiahou Wei-zhen replied in an archaic tone.

He raised no objection. He accepted her reasoning as just.

His face glowed with agreement as he named two warriors under his Forest-Treading Squad.

Steel-Cutting Blade Wanderer Huo Yoon-cheol.

Deep Chivalry Fist Gongye Mi-ryeong.

Jeong Yeon-shin secured promises for two white-rank warriors.

He also learned how their transfer from the Forest-Treading Squad to the Radiant Demon Wing would proceed.

A request would need to be submitted to the administrative office for formal processing, he was told.

"The big guy will handle that. Jeong Yeon-shin, just have the kids below prepare a feast. Think about how to train your lineage too. That's quite a headache. We had plenty of dimwits in the Divine Sword Squad who couldn't follow along. A Leader's prime virtue is patience — keep that in mind."

With Yue Shou-lin's smiling advice, a crimson sky unfurled overhead.

Beneath the dimming twilight.

They parted ways, anticipating a reunion at the Wish for Peace Sword Hall — the Leaders' council.

Desolate Fortress's vastness scattered each contingent's quarters far and wide.

As their shadows stretched in unison across the ground, Jeong Yeon-shin finally set off toward the Radiant Demon Wing pavilion.

"Who will I see first?" he wondered.

The faces of his seniors and subordinates flashed through his mind.

His chest warmed.

***

***

Before the Radiant Demon Wing's grand training ground.

The dusk's stillness shattered. An untimely clamor erupted.

Energy waves and voices interwove in a riot of sound, radiating a vibrance brighter than light itself.

"Leader! This one's reached the seventh level of the Eternal Blossom Fist! I can seamlessly blend Advancing Thunder and Blossoming Fist now!"

"We waited ages after hearing from that Hwangbo fool!"

"Our Desolate Divine Hero must've been worn out from the journey and gone straight to bed..."

"Brother Yeon-shin — no, Leader...!"

"Tell us your exploits! They say you turned the Martial Alliance upside down!"

All manner of seniors emerged to greet him. Blue-ranks and white-ranks, even young unnamed disciples, rushed out to clutch at Jeong Yeon-shin's legs.

Faces beaming with joy welcomed their Leader home.

Off to one side, Lazy Flame Dragon stood chewing poppies, one leg bent.

He spoke to an unnamed disciple beside him.

"Hey, when's the next promotion ceremony?"

"Though younger, I'm your senior. Use honorifics, and I'll answer. Swallow that stuff first — it's only manners."

"..."

"So cool..."

A ten-year-old boy in disciple robes muttered, staring at the black-robed new Leader surrounded by people.

Ignoring Lazy Flame Dragon with brazen audacity — a trivial matter in the Radiant Demon Wing.

At that moment.

"Everyone, let go and head inside."

A woman in blue robes stepped forward. A swordswoman with cool eyes, her blade gleaming with an even colder sheen.

The aura cloaking her carried a heavy air.

Baek Mi-ryeo, the Bewitching Sword Demon — renowned alongside Blue Eyed Demon Sword Cheong Myeong for both beauty and swordsmanship.

"Senior Baek," Jeong Yeon-shin thought.

Warmth touched his expression.

The moment their eyes met, Baek Mi-ryeo's gaze softened too.

"The Leader's mission was long; he should rest first. Haven't you heard the Alliance tales enough? Don't fuss — clear out. We'll catch up tomorrow."

Her voice rang clear, its even tone a hallmark of the Bewitching Sword Demon.

Familiar speech accompanied it.

"Ah," Jeong Yeon-shin realized.

He was finally home.

Tomorrow's Breaking Guide announcement, the administrative office's reward, and duels with Leaders to fill vacancies — all of it faded from his mind for one brief moment.

A strange sensation arose.

A soft feeling, like bedding, seemed to envelop his thoughts.

Then, those who had instantly heeded Baek Mi-ryeo turned away. The elite contingent, equal to Plum Blossom Swordsmen, judged swiftly.

Over a dozen moved in unison. Some nodded, others offered well-wishes.

"I'll look forward to tomorrow's lessons. Honestly, the Leader hasn't really led yet, so it's still a bit awkward..."

"Good enough that you're unharmed. Sort the documents and assign missions. Tomorrow, of course."

"I want to hear your exploits! How was Taoist Cheon Ju's Shadowless Plum Stillness? How did you counter it, Leader?"

"Leader... put in my promotion request. Straight to blue-rank, if possible."

Lazy Flame Dragon, hands clasped behind his neck, shuffled off too.

The Radiant Demon Wing pavilion cluster regained its silence. Darkness settled over the twilight.

A crescent moon, curling against the night sky, cast its faint glow.

The clamor of Radiant Demon Wing masters vanished into a deep stillness.

Within it, Baek Mi-ryeo and Jeong Yeon-shin's footsteps echoed side by side. Rustle, rustle — the sound of treading home ground.

"Cheong Myeong is waiting," Baek Mi-ryeo said quietly.

She and Cheong Myeong, Hyeon Won-chang and Jeong Yeon-shin — the four of them formed the Radiant Demon Quartet. Jeong Yeon-shin had shared his life and his condition with them.

Back when Lady Zhu Lianting of Desolate Ma Family had pressed him.

After rising to black-rank, he had permitted them to speak casually, as seniors and peers. They had earned that right.

"I'm glad you're safe. I was worried. Facing Taoist Cheon Ju, Yeon Hwa-ryeon, and the Sword Star in succession."

Her voice melded with the moonlight.

Cheong Myeong was like an elder brother, Baek Mi-ryeo a sister.

Jeong Yeon-shin held his reply. Feeling their care was enough. He wanted it that way.

Silence lingered from the training ground through the quarters all the way to the Radiant Demon Wing Leader's office.

For them, it was time enough. Though Desolate Fortress law held no formal mentorship, the Radiant Demon Wing's sibling bond was real.

They crossed the tranquil pavilion's threshold.

Step.

Entering the office, Jeong Yeon-shin inhaled a familiar scent.

Iron and oil mingled with aged ink. Familiar fumes drifted over marble-carved furniture.

The smell of steel. Once, Ma Jin's many blades had hung on the wall; now, Desolate Demon treasures filled it.

Over ten swords.

"I wasn't too arrogant to greet you. I hope our Leader knows that. Acting Leader isn't a small task."

A handsome man in a blue turban lounged, skimming documents.

Sharp features, a gentle demeanor. His formless energy wave flowed freely, the turban's tail swaying without end.

Ever a mystical air about him.

"Sorting missions for the crew, rejecting other squads' secondment requests, dueling those leaders who fight over personnel, fudging ledgers for the administrative budget — it's a lot."

"..."

"And this — someone wants to borrow Shin So-bin briefly. But she's teaching the unnamed disciples, so it's a no go. I have to write a polite refusal to keep the heat low. If they're angry, their Leader can send a duel challenge. What can we do? It's not an unreasonable request, so the administrative office can't intervene."

"...Your efforts have been great."

Jeong Yeon-shin praised his senior subordinate with care.

Only then did Cheong Myeong look up. Jade-blue eyes crinkled with a smile.

Noble clan-like, he seemed unburdened by fatigue, yet his clear voice told another story.

"We're half-ruined. Battered here and there. Desolate Fortress is vast, with a survival-of-the-fittest streak, and right now, Radiant Demon Wing is the weakest link. No one's been blatantly thuggish about it, of course, but the pressure is real. We've had to shrink ourselves."

Baek Mi-ryeo, beside him, nodded faintly.

This was the Radiant Demon Wing's reality. The reason Jeong Yeon-shin had sought to bolster its masters before returning.

The Divine Sword Group's seventeen squads were inherently competitive. Even promotions reflected this.

A grand faction like Desolate Fortress was a world unto itself.

A contingent's prestige was one's own. So too the martial might of the Leader they served.

The new Radiant Demon Wing Leader parted his lips slowly.

"Prepare duel challenges. And a list of Leaders still at headquarters."

"Hmm...?"

"I'll send them to the black-rank seniors."

Jeong Yeon-shin touched the hilt of Northern Light, his divine sword.
